features.title: One Ghost, real decisions
features.body: Your Ghost is the commander and failure point. You can build a small squadron of robotic fighters, tamed Zerg, or resurrected Zerg, but they all die if your Ghost dies.

features.title: Days are your chance to breathe
features.body: Scout the jungle, harvest mineral/gas trickles, craft weapons, and set up a temporary safe pocket before night returns.

features.title: Nights are when plans get tested
features.body: The swarm pushes harder after dark. Stay together, protect your lights, and decide when holding ground is no longer worth it.

features.title: Hunt neural critters
features.body: Rare neural critters bolt away from nearby Ghosts and vanish into the jungle if chased poorly. Stun them first, then close in to harvest their valuable neural biomass before they recover.

features.title: Builds stay personal
features.body: Craft weapons and upgrade your Ghost into the role you want: sniper, close-range brawler, explosives specialist, support survivor, or something in between.

features.title: Craft tactical constructs
features.body: Buildables are one craft category: lights, walls, stations, turrets, companion tools, and deployable traps like Maelstrom Mines that burrow and stun nearby enemies.

features.title: Death hurts without ending the run
features.body: If you die, you respawn somewhere in the jungle. You lose most minerals, structures, and companion units, but keep crafted weapons and upgrades.

features.title: The jungle does not stay still
features.body: Enemies evolve over time and pressure comes in waves. Safe spots are temporary, so the squad has to keep adapting.

evolutions.name: Black Forager
evolutions.change: Low HP scout-worker. Mostly tests player aim and reveals where the swarm is spreading.

evolutions.name: Brown Gnawer
evolutions.change: Gains more HP and a faster bite, making ignored worker packs dangerous.

evolutions.name: Orange Stripper
evolutions.change: Gains bonus damage against structures and deconstruct targets, punishing static defenses.

evolutions.name: Red Ravager
evolutions.change: Fast raider with high structure damage and enough HP to slip through weak perimeter fire.

evolutions.name: Black Brood
evolutions.change: Very fragile melee flood unit that appears in numbers.

evolutions.name: Brown Brood
evolutions.change: More movement speed and HP, better at surrounding isolated Ghosts.

evolutions.name: Orange Brood
evolutions.change: Much faster attack cooldown; dangerous if allowed to stack on a player.

evolutions.name: Red Brood
evolutions.change: Tiny berserker variant with high speed and burst damage, forcing quick repositioning.

evolutions.name: Black Skitter
evolutions.change: Basic fast melee enemy: low HP, low damage, short vision.

evolutions.name: Brown Skitter
evolutions.change: Gains +HP and movement speed, becoming reliable chase pressure.

evolutions.name: Orange Ripper
evolutions.change: Switches to a sharper claw profile: higher damage and shorter cooldown.

evolutions.name: Red Ripper
evolutions.change: Elite hunter with max speed, high damage, and enough durability to punish wounded players.

evolutions.name: Black Spitter
evolutions.change: Short-range spine attack with modest damage.

evolutions.name: Brown Spitter
evolutions.change: Gains HP and +1 weapon range, letting it fire from behind melee mobs.

evolutions.name: Orange Needler
evolutions.change: Swaps to a faster needle weapon with better cooldown and higher damage.

evolutions.name: Red Needler
evolutions.change: Long-range elite shooter with high damage, forcing players to break line of sight or snipe it first.

evolutions.name: Black Burrower
evolutions.change: Slow setup ambusher with low damage spikes.

evolutions.name: Brown Burrower
evolutions.change: More HP and stronger spine damage, holding narrow paths better.

evolutions.name: Orange Impaler
evolutions.change: Longer spine range and higher splash pressure, threatening grouped players.

evolutions.name: Red Impaler
evolutions.change: Elite area-control unit with very long reach and lethal line damage if not cleared quickly.

evolutions.name: Black Matron
evolutions.change: Light support threat with low HP and short-range harassment.

evolutions.name: Brown Matron
evolutions.change: Gains HP and faster movement so it can stay behind the front line.

evolutions.name: Orange Hexer
evolutions.change: Switches to a longer-range disruption weapon/effect to pressure priority targets.

evolutions.name: Red Hexer
evolutions.change: High-priority support unit with long range and punishing debuff-style pressure.

evolutions.name: Black Wing
evolutions.change: Light air threat with low HP and short-range poke.

evolutions.name: Brown Wing
evolutions.change: Gains movement speed and HP, making it harder to kite.

evolutions.name: Orange Razorwing
evolutions.change: Upgraded bouncing glaive damage for harassment against grouped players.

evolutions.name: Red Razorwing
evolutions.change: Fast elite flyer with higher damage and longer range, demanding anti-air weapon swaps.

evolutions.name: Black Siegewing
evolutions.change: Slow air artillery with low damage but long enough range to teach target priority.

evolutions.name: Brown Siegewing
evolutions.change: Gains HP and stronger siege damage against structures.

evolutions.name: Orange Shellwing
evolutions.change: Longer range and higher impact damage, forcing the squad to move or counter-snipe.

evolutions.name: Red Shellwing
evolutions.change: Elite siege threat with very long range and heavy structure/player pressure.

evolutions.name: Black Bloater
evolutions.change: Slow fragile special unit with a small death warning.

evolutions.name: Brown Bloater
evolutions.change: Gains HP and a larger death burst radius.

evolutions.name: Orange Plaguebearer
evolutions.change: Death effect becomes plague-style damage over an area, punishing close-range kills.

evolutions.name: Red Plaguebearer
evolutions.change: Large plague/explosion death effect that can break grouped players if killed carelessly.

evolutions.name: Black Crusher
evolutions.change: Slow tank with high HP but low early damage.

evolutions.name: Brown Crusher
evolutions.change: Gains armor and a larger HP pool, requiring sustained fire.

evolutions.name: Orange Mauler
evolutions.change: Gains speed and a stronger melee weapon, becoming a real chase threat.

evolutions.name: Red Mauler
evolutions.change: Boss-like elite with heavy armor, huge HP, and punishing melee damage.

upgrades.title: Weapon unlocks
upgrades.body: Level 1 in a weapon path unlocks the craft. Once unlocked, that weapon becomes part of your permanent arsenal and can be equipped again later.

upgrades.title: Weapon switching
upgrades.body: You can only run one main crafted weapon at a time, but you can switch back to any weapon you have previously crafted instead of losing access.

upgrades.title: Rifle path
upgrades.body: Reliable single-target damage. Further upgrades can improve damage, cooldown, range, and passive attack effects.

upgrades.title: Shotgun path
upgrades.body: Short-range burst weapon. Upgrades can add stronger close damage, wider hit patterns, or attack-linked knockback/control effects.

upgrades.title: Sniper path
upgrades.body: Long-range, slower cooldown, high damage. Later upgrades can emphasize elite picking, bonus range, or high-value target passives.

upgrades.title: Flamer or acid path
upgrades.body: Area-control weapon concept. Upgrades can create lingering zones, damage-over-time pressure, or debuffs tied to basic attacks.

upgrades.title: Explosive path
upgrades.body: Anti-pack splash option. Upgrades can tune radius, splash falloff, friendly-risk tradeoffs, and burst timing.

upgrades.title: Survival: max health
upgrades.body: Raises Ghost durability so death is less sudden, especially during night collapses or failed relocations.

upgrades.title: Survival: armor and shields
upgrades.body: Adds defensive layers such as armor, shield capacity, and possible shield regeneration.

upgrades.title: Mobility and vision
upgrades.body: Improves movement speed and sight range so players can scout, kite, regroup, and escape bad holds more cleanly.

upgrades.title: Harvest efficiency
upgrades.body: Improves resource trickle and reduces downtime when gathering from trees, rocks, crystals, bushes, and organic growth.

upgrades.title: Construct utility
upgrades.body: Improves temporary structures, deconstruction refunds, repair value, alerts, and deployable trap support.

upgrades.title: Maelstrom Mines
upgrades.body: Burrowed traps that stun nearby enemies when triggered. Upgrades can change arm time, radius, duration, or elite interrupt value.

upgrades.title: Companion unlocks
upgrades.body: Craft robotic fighters, tame stunned or weakened Zerg, or resurrect Zerg from corpses/biomass into a small support squad.

upgrades.title: Companion upgrades
upgrades.body: Improve companion count, durability, or roles. They add tactical power, but still die when the Ghost dies.

gameplayLoop.title: Survive five nights in the jungle
gameplayLoop.body: Victory comes after surviving five nights in the jungle. The current target is a five-night run, potentially ending with a final boss before extraction or victory. Endless mode can layer on later for players who want to keep pushing.

gameplayLoop.title: Scavenge by day, endure the night
gameplayLoop.body: Pressure comes in waves instead of constant attacks. The 256x256 map is divided into 16x16-tile spawn areas; each area watches player presence, light coverage, and mob density before spawning or draining pressure. Nights raise the respawn pressure.

gameplayLoop.title: Command one Ghost and a small squad
gameplayLoop.body: Each player controls one Ghost hero as the commander. Over time, they can craft robotic fighters, tame Zerg, or resurrect Zerg into a small controllable squadron. The squad adds tactical options, but it is tied to the Ghost.

gameplayLoop.title: Harvest minerals and vespene over time
gameplayLoop.body: Trees, bushes, rocks, crystals, and organic growth all produce both minerals and vespene gas while a Ghost keeps harvesting. Every few seconds, resources trickle in; each node type has its own mineral/gas bias.

gameplayLoop.title: Stun neural critters before they flee
gameplayLoop.body: Some neutral jungle critters are valuable but skittish. They run away from Ghosts, so players need a stun window or coordinated body-block before harvesting their neural biomass. Letting them recover gives them a chance to escape.

gameplayLoop.title: Craft one active weapon at a time
gameplayLoop.body: Weapon paths are unlocked by buying level 1, which grants access to craft that weapon. After that you can keep upgrading the path, and you can switch back to any previously crafted weapon while still equipping only one main weapon at a time.

gameplayLoop.title: Build safe pockets, then abandon them
gameplayLoop.body: Light sources gradually brighten the nearby screen and suppress mob spawning in covered 16x16 areas. Walls, regeneration stations, turrets, and Maelstrom Mines help the squad hold ground — but death destroys your built constructs, and the map pressures you to move.

gameplayLoop.title: Death hurts, but progression survives
gameplayLoop.body: When a Ghost dies, the player respawns at a random position. They lose most carried resources, all structures they built, and all companion units, but keep upgrades and unlocked progression.

gameplayLoop.title: The swarm evolves with time
gameplayLoop.body: Enemy evolution is mainly time-based, but player actions like heavy killing or deep exploration can accelerate the threat. The map scales for 1–5 players, rewarding collaboration because the squad is stronger together.

resources.title: Trees
resources.body: Common daytime harvest targets with a mineral-heavy trickle and a little vespene. Good for steady early income while scouting safer edges.

resources.title: Bushes
resources.body: Fast, small organic harvest targets. They trickle modest minerals and slightly better gas than trees, rewarding movement and side-path checks.

resources.title: Rocks
resources.body: Slow, mineral-rich nodes with low gas output. Best taken when the squad has secured enough light or cover for a longer harvest.

resources.title: Crystals
resources.body: High-value vespene-rich finds with a smaller mineral trickle. They create a reason to push deeper into risky jungle territory.

resources.title: Organic growth
resources.body: Organic growth produces a balanced mineral/gas trickle and is often found closer to danger.

resources.title: Neural critters
resources.body: Fast neutral wildlife that runs from players. Stun or trap them before harvesting; if they recover, they may sprint away and despawn.

resources.title: Harvesting tradeoff
resources.body: Right-click to begin continuous harvesting. Every few seconds minerals and vespene trickle in until the node depletes, the Ghost is interrupted, or the player moves. While harvesting, your Ghost cannot fire.

notes.title: Reuse unused abilities as spells
notes.body: Audit dormant StarCraft abilities and rebuild them into simple commando spells where possible. Prefer effects players can read instantly: burst damage, area control, debuffs, rescue tools, and squad utility.

notes.title: Command card stays readable
notes.body: Most player power should come from passive attack-linked abilities instead of a crowded hotbar. The command card should be obvious to casual players: attack, move, build/interact, and only a few active choices.

notes.title: Few active hotbar abilities
notes.body: Keep active abilities focused around Heal, Speed, and Stun. These can upgrade over the run, and higher tiers may change behavior instead of only adding numbers.

notes.title: Psi Emitter feasibility
notes.body: Investigate whether a Psi Emitter-style construct can be built from scratch. Best candidate: a deployable lure/beacon that redirects Zerg pressure, creates a risky hold point, or powers nearby spell/passive effects.
